description_long: This hammer features a robust, angular stone head with a faceted, geometric design, giving it a primitive yet functional appearance. The head is securely fastened to a rugged, irregularly shaped wooden handle using crisscrossed bindings, likely made of leather or cord, which wrap around the upper portion of the handle and the base of the head. The handle itself is dark brown with a marbled texture, suggesting it is crafted from a dense, natural wood. The stone head is a light beige color, contrasting sharply with the darker handle, and appears to be roughly hewn rather than polished. The bindings are a muted greenish-tan, adding a utilitarian touch and reinforcing the connection between the head and handle. There are no visible markings, logos, or signs of wear, and the hammer appears to be in excellent condition.
description: A primitive-style hammer with a faceted stone head bound to a rugged, marbled wooden handle using crisscrossed bindings. The head is light beige, while the handle is dark brown with a natural, irregular shape.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed end of the stone head and the narrow profile of the handle, with bindings visible at the top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full width of the angular stone head and the crisscrossed bindings securing it to the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the stone head and the marbled texture of the handle, similar to the front but with less detail on the bindings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the geometric facets of the stone head and the way the bindings wrap around the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Irregular, marbled wooden handle with a natural, slightly curved shape and visible texture; wrapped at the top with crisscrossed bindings for grip and reinforcement.
attribute_head_type: Faceted, geometric stone head with a primitive, roughly hewn design; no claws or peens, resembling a basic sledge or club head.
